name: Consus
description: Consus was an obscure, ancient Roman god whose functions were eventually obscured and debated. He was primarily associated with grain storage and protection, particularly the grain stored underground. Some scholars believe he was an archaic god of the harvest, representing the hidden potential of seeds and crops buried in the earth. His festival, the Consualia, was celebrated twice a year, on August 21 and December 15, and involved chariot races and other agricultural rituals. This festival marked the end of harvest and the beginning of sowing season, connecting Consus to both the abundance of the harvest and the promise of future crops. An underground altar dedicated to Consus was located in the Circus Maximus, only exposed during the Consualia festivals. The secrecy surrounding his altar and rituals suggests a connection to chthonic or underworld deities and mysteries. He was also associated with horses and mules, which were adorned with flowers during the Consualia, possibly signifying their importance in agriculture and transportation. Some Roman writers linked Consus to the abduction of the Sabine women, suggesting that he advised Romulus to hold games and festivals to attract neighboring populations, resulting in the infamous event. The true nature and origins of Consus remain uncertain, but his association with agriculture, hidden potential, and the cycle of seasons makes him a fascinating figure in Roman religious history.
subtitle: God of Grain Storage and Counsel

keyMyths.title: The Consualia Festival
keyMyths.description: The Consualia was celebrated twice a year with chariot races and sacrifices, honoring Consus and the harvest. It marked the end of the harvest and the beginning of sowing season.

keyMyths.title: Consus and the Sabine Women
keyMyths.description: Some accounts connect Consus to Romulus's plan to abduct the Sabine women, suggesting he advised Romulus to hold games and attract neighboring populations. This event led to the integration of the Sabines into Roman society.

keyMyths.title: The Underground Altar
keyMyths.description: Consus's altar was located underground in the Circus Maximus, only exposed during the Consualia festivals. This suggests a connection to chthonic deities and hidden potential.
